sources.name: WordNet — Cognitive Register and Synset Depth
sources.note: Synset depth used to estimate cognitive load by abstraction level

_description: Tags every word class by cognitive load. Four register levels from concrete to ai_signature. The pipeline reads this file at step 4 after domain detection to assign a register ceiling. No word above the ceiling may appear in output unless domain_exceptions.json permits it.

registers.description: Physical, observable, directly sensory. No domain knowledge required. A reader at any level understands these words immediately.
Common concepts understood by most adults without specialist training. Present in everyday speech and general writing. The default ceiling for most output domains.
Domain knowledge required to decode meaning. A general reader cannot reliably interpret these words without context or training. Permitted only when domain tag matches.
Words that appear at anomalously high rates in LLM output relative to human writing corpora. They add no meaning, signal model-generated text, and reduce trust. Always blocked regardless of domain.

words.note: Overused in LLM output. Carries no meaning beyond 'strong' or 'reliable'.
Rare in human writing. Anomalously frequent in LLM output.
Used as filler. If nuance exists, describe it — do not label it.
Business jargon with no added meaning over 'use'.
Inflated modifier. Replace with the specific claim it makes.
No precise meaning. Replace with what is actually included.
Filler phrase. If the point matters, state it directly.
Vague. Name the actual group: staff, residents, contractors, patients.
Nominalization trap. Replace with the direct verb.
Overused in LLM output where 'show' is sufficient.
Transitional filler. If the point follows logically, no connector is needed.
The conclusion is the last paragraph. Label not needed.
Vague summary word. State the specific claim instead.
Formal filler. Replace with the shorter connector or rewrite.
